denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received a prior authorization request to transfer you from acute hospital care to an Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility (IRF). We are denying this request.

Based on UnitedHealthcare guidelines, admission to an IRF requires evidence that the patient can actively participate in and benefit from at least three (3) hours of intensive therapy per day. The clinical notes from your physical therapist in the hospital indicate you fatigued after 30 minutes of therapy yesterday. Therefore, we are authorizing a transfer to a Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF) instead, which provides a lower, less intensive level of care.
clinical_context: Patient had a severe stroke but is highly motivated. The single day she fatigued after 30 minutes was the day immediately following her stroke. Since then, she has been doing 2 hours a day easily, but the hospital submitted the older notes. An SNF will not provide the intensive daily therapy needed for her to regain walking ability.
